title: Advanced Project
summary: Build a production-grade REST API with async patterns, testing, database, and Docker deployment

sections.title: Full-stack Python — from idea to deployed API
sections.context: This capstone project ties together everything you've learned: OOP design, async concurrency, testing, databases, API design, error handling, and deployment. You'll build a task management API (like a simplified Trello) with FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, async endpoints, pytest tests, and Docker deployment. The project is larger than previous ones, but you'll build it in layers: first the data model, then the API endpoints, then tests, then Docker. Each layer builds on the previous one. By the end, you'll have a production-ready web service you could deploy to the cloud. This mirrors real-world Python development where you own a feature end-to-end.

sections.title: Async CRUD endpoints with FastAPI and SQLAlchemy
sections.content: A task management API with create, read, and list endpoints
sections.code:
# main.py — FastAPI application entry point
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Depends  # Web framework
from pydantic import BaseModel  # Request/response validation
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base, Session, sessionmaker

# Pydantic model for request validation — enforces types automatically
class TaskCreate(BaseModel):
    title: str  # Required string — FastAPI rejects missing/invalid
    description: str = ""  # Optional with default empty string

class TaskResponse(BaseModel):
    id: int
    title: str
    description: str
    completed: bool

# Database setup — SQLite for simplicity, PostgreSQL for production
DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./tasks.db"  # File-based database
engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, connect_args={"check_same_thread": False})
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)

app = FastAPI(title="Task Manager API")  # Create the FastAPI application

def get_db():
    # Dependency that provides a database session to each request
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db  # Session is provided to the endpoint
    finally:
        db.close()  # Always close the session after request completes

@app.post("/tasks/", response_model=TaskResponse)
def create_task(task: TaskCreate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    db_task = TaskModel(title=task.title, description=task.description)
    db.add(db_task)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_task)
    return db_task

@app.get("/tasks/", response_model=list[TaskResponse])
def list_tasks(db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    return db.query(TaskModel).all()

# Run with: uvicorn main:app --reload --port 8000
# Test: curl http://localhost:8000/tasks/

sections.title: Project architecture — beyond the code
sections.context: A professional project has structure beyond just working code. Organize by layers: models/ (database models), schemas/ (Pydantic models), routers/ (API endpoints), services/ (business logic), and tests/. Use dependency injection for database sessions, authentication, and configuration. Add request logging with structlog for production debugging. Set up CI/CD (GitHub Actions) to run tests and deploy on every push to main. Use environment variables for all configuration — never hard-code database URLs or secret keys. Document your API with auto-generated docs (FastAPI provides /docs automatically). Implement proper HTTP status codes (201 for created, 404 for not found, 422 for validation errors).

sections.title: Don't put business logic in endpoint functions
sections.context: Fat endpoint functions with business logic mixed into request handling are untestable. Wrong: @app.post(...) directly computes tax, saves to DB, sends email — all in 50 lines. Correct: endpoint calls service layer: service.create_order(data). Service calls repository: repo.save(order). Each layer is independently testable. Endpoints handle HTTP concerns (status codes, serialization). Services handle business rules. Repositories handle data access. A change to tax calculation means editing the service, not the route.

sections.title: Your turn
sections.prompt: Build the complete task manager API with these files: (1) models.py — SQLAlchemy Task model with id, title, description, completed, created_at, (2) schemas.py — Pydantic models for TaskCreate, TaskUpdate, TaskResponse, (3) main.py — FastAPI app with CRUD endpoints (create, list, get by id, update, delete), (4) test_api.py — pytest tests using TestClient from fastapi.testclient, testing all 5 CRUD operations, (5) Dockerfile — multi-stage build, (6) docker-compose.yml — app + database services. The update endpoint should support partial updates (only modify fields that are provided).
hints: How do you use FastAPI's TestClient to test endpoints without a running server?
What HTTP methods correspond to each CRUD operation (create, read, update, delete)?
